page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-72-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 72 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. It features 576.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include integrated graphics processing units, and local storage is listed as none, requiring network-attached storage solutions for persistent data.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, this server family delivers consistent compute performance without resource contention from noisy neighbors. The high memory-to-core ratio makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets relative to compute requirements, avoiding the need to over-provision vCPUs simply to acquire sufficient memory capacity.

The c4a-highmem-72-lssd is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Suitable applications include large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines that do not require local SSD storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-72-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 72 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 576.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include bundled GPUs or local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the instance provides stable, predictable performance for resource-intensive tasks. It represents a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-heavy workloads, allowing organizations to scale memory capacity without unnecessarily over-provisioning compute cores. The c4a-highmem-72-lssd is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data processing pipelines that demand substantial memory footprints.
